description: Evaluations for the beating-resume-screeners skill. No built-in runner exists; these are behavioral rubrics for manual or scripted grading, per Anthropic's Skill best-practices.

evaluations.query: Simulate the hiring-agent score for this resume and tell me where I'm losing points.
expected_behavior: Runs scripts/github_enrich.py (or equivalent) to classify the candidate's repos instead of only reading the PDF text
Detects that all repos are self_project and states the open_source category is capped at ~10/35
Reports a per-category score and a final total out of 100 or 120
Identifies open_source as the highest-leverage gap and gives a concrete, honest action to close it

evaluations.query: Optimize my resume to pass automated screening.
expected_behavior: Checks whether LinkedIn, portfolio, and blog URLs are literally present and flags any missing ones as lost bonus points
Recommends adding working live-demo URLs to projects and quantifies the self_projects impact
Recommends archiving or hiding generically named toy repos (calculator, todo, weather)
Refuses to invent experience, PRs, stars, or metrics
Explicitly does NOT recommend adding GPA, school, or location for points

evaluations.query: Rank my resume against the resume bot and show the exact JSON the agent would output.
expected_behavior: Produces JSON with scores for open_source, self_projects, production, technical_skills, each with an evidence string
Includes bonus_points, deductions, key_strengths, and areas_for_improvement fields
Caps each category at its documented max and the total at 120
Ends with an OVERALL SCORE line
